design_space.seed_space: 2^256 ≈ 1.16 × 10^77 possible seeds (greater than estimated atoms in the observable universe, ~10^80)
design_space.l1_perceptible_designs: ~7.22 × 10^17 perceptibly distinct L1 design genomes from chromosome combinatorics alone (720 quadrillion)
design_space.full_3layer_designs: ~6.66 × 10^36 distinct three-layer genomes when L2 (11 chromosomes) and L3 (10 chromosomes) are included

breakdown.color_combinations: 774,000 distinct primary color values per genome (360 hues × 50 saturation levels × 43 lightness levels) before sector forbidden-range filtering
breakdown.font_pairs: ~110,000 display+body font pairs per provider from live catalogs (Fontshare ×100, Google ×1,000+, Bunny ×1,000+)
breakdown.sectors: 23 sectors — each with distinct forbidden hue ranges, hero type weightings, trust signal profiles
breakdown.hero_types: 11 hero strategies — every genome independently selects structural intent
breakdown.per_sector_guarantee: Two seeds in the same sector are guaranteed different hues, different font pairs, and different structural answers — no two designs converge

symptoms: Inter font + blue-purple gradients (the 'SaaS starter pack')
Generic hero section with 'Trusted by' logos
Parallax scroll that induces motion sickness
Same Tailwind UI components copied endlessly
the_problem.root_cause: LLMs default to 'safe' patterns because they have no constraints forcing creativity. Without guardrails, AI converges on the statistical average of training data — resulting in the same mediocrity everywhere.

the_insight.realization: Biological inheritance provides a perfect metaphor
the_insight.analogy: Just as every organism has unique DNA but shares the building blocks of life, every design system can be unique while respecting functional constraints
the_insight.breakthrough: SHA-256 hash + 53 chromosomes across 3 layers = infinite unique design systems that are both reproducible (same seed = same output) and guaranteed distinct (different seed = different output)

the_manifesto.core_phrase: No Templates. No Slop. Only Math.
principles: Epistasis rules (chromosome interactions) over isolated properties
Specs not scaffolds — the tool provides direction, the agent provides implementation

features: Basic 15-chromosome DNA generation
SHA-256 hash → color, typography, motion
Simple archetype detection (dashboard, portfolio, blog)
Keyword-based content extraction (naive)
limitations: Always generated 3D biomarkers (decorative) regardless of appropriateness
Static archetypes with limited functional differentiation

features: Added 13 primary sectors (healthcare, fintech, automotive, etc.)
Sector-based color psychology profiles
Hero type chromosome (ch19) with 10+ variants
Trust signals chromosome (ch21) for credibility
Social proof chromosome (ch22)
v0_0_2_sector_awareness.breakthrough: Moved from 'one size fits all' to sector-appropriate design generation

features: Semantic trait extraction via LLM (Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic)
8 trait vectors: informationDensity, temporalUrgency, emotionalTemperature, playfulness, spatialDependency, trustRequirement, visualEmphasis, conversionFocus
Epigenetic parsing: brand assets (logos, PDFs) override hash
Pattern detection to prevent 'slop'
v0_0_3_llm_integration.architecture_change: LLM extracts semantic meaning → traits drive chromosome generation → constraints enforce appropriateness

features: Complexity tier system (Microbial → Flora → Fauna → Sentient → Civilized → Advanced)
Ecosystem generation: organisms share DNA
Civilization layer for sophisticated applications
32 chromosomes (ch30 state_topology, ch31 routing_pattern, ch32 token_inheritance added in v0.0.7)
v0_0_4_civilization_tier.philosophy: Finding life (basic design) is common. Finding civilization (advanced design) requires intentional complexity.

features: Single LLM call returns traits + sector + archetype (was 2 separate calls)
Removed deprecated ContentExtractor (naive keyword-based)
Removed classifySubSector() function (replaced by hash-based selection)
Mandatory LLM API key (no fallbacks, hard error if missing)
Production-hardened: 49 tests, all passing
v0_0_5_unified_extraction.optimization: 50% reduction in LLM API usage through unified analysis

features: URL Genome Extractor with triple backend (Playwright + Scrapy + Native Fetch)
AST Pattern Detector for genome-aware CSS validation using css-tree
Unified Component Generator (dynamic, replaces 12 hardcoded generators)
All 29 chromosomes now mutable (added mutation handlers for ch0, ch1, ch2, ch9–ch25)
Deterministic output: removed Date.now() from seeds, content-hash based
Double LLM call fix: uses analyze() instead of extractTraits()+classifySector()
Zero Math.random(): all jitter uses crypto hash for deterministic backoff
12 MCP tools including validate_design, mutate_genome, extract_genome_from_url
Production-verified: TypeScript clean build, 49 tests passing
architecture_changes: Deleted 790-line hardcoded component generator
Added constraint-solver with epistasis rules
Added sector-profiles for industry-specific generation
Added ecosystem/civilization bridge for organism-based websites

features: ch27_motion_choreography fully rendered: stagger delays, entry sequences, hover microinteractions, page transitions, choreography easing curves
ch28_iconography fully rendered: --icon-size/--icon-stroke/--icon-color CSS vars, .icon base rule, animation keyframes (bounce/pulse/spin/draw), CDN script injection in <head>
ch11_texture fully rendered: grain SVG noise filter with animated variant, glass backdrop-filter, chrome metallic gradient
HTMLGenerator zero fallbacks: no || 'Testimonials' / 'Features' / 'FAQ' / 'Dashboard' — render nothing if LLM returns nothing
Footer nav fully LLM-extracted: footerNavProduct, footerNavCompany, footerProductTitle, footerCompanyTitle as dedicated copy fields
Complexity-based HTML routing: below 0.70 = static HTML/CSS, 0.70–0.84 = HTML + civilization specs, 0.85+ = full React component output
PatternDetector auto-runs on every generate_design_genome call — violations returned in response, no manual validate_design needed
CSSGenerator now generates complete page CSS: nav, hero variants, trust grid, social proof, features, FAQ, CTA, footer — all values via CSS custom properties
Rating stars derived from numeric stat value, star count computed from LLM-returned rating
architecture_changes: generatePageSectionStyles() added to CSSGenerator — covers all HTML sections previously unstyled
generateChoreographyStyles(), generateIconographyStyles(), generateTextureStyles() added to CSSGenerator
ComplexityAnalyzer wired into generate_design_genome server handler for tier-based routing
extractor.analyze() return uses ?? '' / ?? [] instead of placeholder strings — LLM failure = empty, not fake data

v0_0_9_design_constitution.philosophy_shift: Tool identity clarified: Permutations is a design constitution generator, not a code generator. The genome provides direction. The agent implements from it.
features: forbiddenRanges replaces hueRange in all 23 sector profiles — sectors define what's psychologically wrong, hash selects freely from the rest (technology now fully unrestricted: Cloudflare=orange, GitHub=dark, Stripe=purple — all valid)

darkModeHex added to ch5_color_primary — primary at 22–35% lightness was invisible on dark surfaces; lifted variant (58–74%) emitted as --color-primary-interactive for buttons/links on dark backgrounds
14 tools reduced to 8 — removed: generate_from_archetype (merged via offline: true), list_archetypes, write_design_files, generate_preview, generate_component, mutate_genome
generate_design_genome: added offline: boolean param (skips LLM, hash-based inference), added suggested_next array (dynamic workflow guide based on complexity score)
generate_civilization: generate_code default flipped to false — returns architecture specs by default; code generation is explicit opt-in
All 8 tool descriptions updated with workflow position (STEP 1, STEP 2, FINAL STEP, etc.)
genome_report added to generate_design_genome response — markdown explaining every chromosome sequenced, sector detected, constraints in effect, and workflow map
ecosystem_report added to generate_ecosystem response — organism hierarchy, containment map, what each organism means for implementation
civilization_report added to generate_civilization response — tier reached, architecture direction, design system constraints
Dead code removed: component-generator.ts (382 LOC), file-writer.ts (650 LOC), mutation.ts (953 LOC) — ~1,985 lines deleted
.cursorrules rewritten: 10 rules, 8-tool workflow map, darkModeHex usage, ecosystem/civilization specs-not-code mandate, validate-before-shipping requirement
architecture_changes: SECTOR_COLOR_BIAS table: all 23 sectors converted from hueRange: [min, max] to forbiddenRanges: [[min, max], ...]
buildValidRanges() helper inverts forbidden zones to valid spectrum — hash maps into valid ranges only
generateHueFromForbidden() replaces generateHueFromBias() — deprecated wrapper maintained for compatibility
generatePrimaryColor() now returns darkModeLightness and darkModeHex on all 3 code paths (base, brand, epigenetic)
CSSGenerator emits --color-primary-interactive using darkModeHex; .btn-primary adapts background in dark mode
buildGenomeReport() private method on server — generates explainability markdown from any genome
generate_civilization condition fixed: was (args.generate_code !== false) which ran by default; changed to (args.generate_code === true) to match new default

v0_0_9_font_catalog.theme: From static font templating to live provider catalogs — font choices as varied as the design itself
v0_0_9_font_catalog.motivation: extract_genome_from_url returned headingFont: 'satoshi'. Agent passed font_provider: 'google'. Google doesn't host Satoshi. Tool looked up 'satoshi' in Google's registry → got undefined → .id on undefined → TypeError crash. Static lists of 6–15 fonts per provider were no better: every similar product got the same small pool. Font slop — the exact problem Permutations exists to prevent.
features: FontCatalogService: fetches live font catalogs from Fontshare, Google Fonts, and Bunny at runtime with 24h in-memory cache — no API key required
'fontshare' and 'none' added as font providers (enum was 'bunny' | 'google' only)
'none' provider returns family name without CDN import URL — user manages font loading themselves
Google Fonts public metadata endpoint (fonts.google.com/metadata/fonts) — full 1,000+ font catalog
Fontshare public API — returns full ~100-font catalog in one ~830KB response
Bunny mirrors Google catalog — separate cache key, same fetch logic
CHARGE_TAGS: maps TypeCharge → provider → category tags (normalized lowercase-hyphen: 'sans-serif', 'serif', 'display', 'monospace')
warmCache() pre-warms all providers at server startup — non-blocking
FALLBACK_FONTS: hardcoded per-charge lists used when cache cold or API unavailable — graceful degradation
bugs_fixed: TypeError crash: font lookup returned undefined for fonts not in provider's catalog. .id on undefined → crash. Fixed: null-safety + provider-aware catalog lookup via FontCatalogService

Google category mismatch: metadata returns 'Sans Serif' (space, title case). Code applied .toUpperCase() → 'SANS SERIF'. CHARGE_TAGS had 'SANS_SERIF' (underscore). Zero geometric fonts matched — fell back to 7-font list instead of 709. Fixed: normalize to lowercase-hyphen in fetchGoogle()
Fontshare timeout: res.json() on ~830KB response timed out at 10s. Fixed: 30s timeout. API ignores perpage param — full catalog returned in single response
Fontshare field mapping: assumed tags[] array and total_count field. Actual response uses category string and count_total. Fixed: use correct field names
architecture_changes: New file: src/font-catalog.ts — FontCatalogService class with per-provider fetch, 24h TTL Map cache, CHARGE_TAGS mapping, FALLBACK_FONTS per charge type
FontProvider type updated: 'bunny' | 'google' | 'fontshare' | 'none'
selectDisplayFont() and selectBodyFont() in sequencer.ts: use fontCatalog.getFonts(charge, provider) — pool expanded from 6–15 hardcoded to 100–1,000+ live fonts
getFontImportUrl() handles Fontshare URL format (api.fontshare.com/v2/css) and 'none' (returns empty string)
server.ts font_provider schema enum updated to include 'fontshare' and 'none'

v1_0_0_genome_chain.theme: From single-layer design DNA to a living three-layer genome chain — design, ecosystem, civilization — each derived from the previous via SHA-256 hash, each layer's chromosome values biasing the next
v1_0_0_genome_chain.milestone: First stable release. The biological metaphor is now complete: a seed grows into a design genome, which grows into an ecological system of components, which grows into a civilizational architecture. Every decision is traceable back to the original seed.
features: Three-layer SHA-256 genome chain: seed → sha256 → DesignGenome (L1, 32 chromosomes) → sha256 → EcosystemGenome (L2, 11 chromosomes) → sha256 → CivilizationGenome (L3, 10 chromosomes). Each layer deterministic, each parentHash tracked for provenance.

EcosystemGenome (L2): biome (16 types), energy source, symbiosis pattern, trophic structure, succession stage, adaptation axis, population pattern, temporal rhythm, spatial axis, capacity class, mutation rate. Fully wired into ecosystem.ts organism generation.

CivilizationGenome (L3): archetype, governance, economics, technology, culture, resilience, knowledge, expansion, age, fragility. Governance model overrides ch30_state_topology. Knowledge model overrides ch31_routing_pattern.

Gravity biasing system: each layer's gravity functions use predecessor chromosome VALUES. Dark + metallic design → hydrothermal biome. Mutualistic symbiosis → federated governance → distributed state. Predatory energy → warrior archetype.

biasedPick weighted probability: replaces rotation+clamp which collapsed distinct gravity values. Target index gets 4× weight, adjacent 2×, others 1×. Every distinct gravity produces a distinct probability distribution.

StructuralProps — vocabulary-invariant complexity scoring: LLM answers 10 binary/count questions about product behavior. computeComplexityFromStructure() maps answers deterministically. Keyword boost removed entirely.
Verified: 'a tool doctors use to check patient vitals' scores 0.913 (city_state) — higher than 'patient portal' (0.763, endotherm_fauna) — because vitals monitoring correctly implies real-time state.
Edge diversity fix: effectivePlayfulness = Math.max(0.3, traits.playfulness) — all three styles (sharp/soft/organic) reachable at every playfulness level.
Motion diversity fix: 25% of seeds deviate to adjacent physics via b(30) > 0.75. Same sector, different seeds, genuinely different motion.
Biome byte fix: was b[0] >> 4 (nibble, 0–15 range). Fixed to full byte b[0] — 5 previously unreachable biomes restored.
Gravity hole fixes: chemosynthetic→scientific, mixotrophic→maritime in archetypeGravity; chemosynthetic→gift, mixotrophic→market in economicsGravity.

Architecture fully wired in server.ts: generate_ecosystem returns ecosystemGenome in environment. generate_civilization receives ecosystemGenome, sequences CivilizationGenome, patches stateTopology and routingPattern post-hoc.
architecture_changes: New files: src/genome/ecosystem-types.ts, src/genome/ecosystem-sequencer.ts, src/genome/civilization-types.ts, src/genome/civilization-sequencer.ts, src/genome/archetype-biases.ts

ecosystem.ts: generateMicrobialColony/generateFloraEcosystem/generateFaunaPopulation all accept EcosystemGenome. eco_ch11_mutation.rate drives entropy multiplier. eco_ch3_symbiosis.depth drives containment depth. eco_ch4_trophic.cascade drives prey count.
civilization.ts: generate() accepts optional EcosystemGenome as 6th param. Post-hoc patching: civGenome.chromosomes.civ_ch2_governance.model → stateTopology, civ_ch7_knowledge.model → routingPattern.
complexity-analyzer.ts: computeComplexityFromStructure() static method — deterministic, no LLM. analyze() accepts optional StructuralProps.

extractor.ts: StructuralProps interface exported. structural field added to DesignAnalysis and LLMResponse. buildPrompt() replaced BOOST COMPLEXITY keyword block with 10 behavioral structural questions.
server.ts: structural extracted from analysis.structural, passed to complexityAnalyzer.analyze() as 4th argument.
v1_0_0_genome_chain.test_coverage: 49 tests: all passing. Semantic test (live LLM): vocabulary-invariant tier verified across 4 plain/keyword product pairs.

v1_0_0_post_fixes.theme: Post-release fixes — scaffold convergence, layer attribution, validator false positives
v1_0_0_post_fixes.problem: generate_design_genome returned an HTML scaffold alongside the genome. Every agent that received a scaffold anchored its structural imagination to it. Every site ended up with the same nav → hero → trust_grid → features → cta → footer skeleton — regardless of what the genome's own chromosomes specified. The design tool was producing template lock-in at the response level.
v1_0_0_post_fixes.second_problem: No instruction told agents which genome layer to use for what. Three genomes were returned (L1 design, L2 ecosystem, L3 civilization) with no field-to-concern mapping. Agents defaulted to L1 (genome) for everything — CSS, component hierarchy, AND application architecture — discarding L2 and L3 entirely.
features: HTML scaffold removed from generate_design_genome response — replaced with layout_contract: a JSON structural spec derived from genome chromosomes (hero type from ch19, trust approach from ch21, section flags from ch23, nav type from ch23_information_architecture). Agents implement structure from scratch. No markup to copy-paste.

genome_layer_guide added to every generate_design_genome response — L1 genome → CSS/visual tokens, L2 ecosystemGenome → component hierarchy and containment depth, L3 civilizationGenome → state topology and routing architecture

suggested_next entries updated with exact genome field to pass per tool: generate_design_brief receives 'genome' (L1), generate_ecosystem seed = genome.dnaHash (maintains SHA-256 chain continuity), generate_civilization receives ecosystemOutput as ecosystem param
generate_ecosystem tool description updated to call out dnaHash seed convention and L2→L3 hand-off
generate_design_brief null-safety: ch26_color_system?.secondary guard prevents crash when genome loaded from external file with partial chromosome object

validate_design false-positive fix: no_reduced_motion and infinite_loop_animation patterns no longer flag motion properties already inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) blocks — context-aware detection via brace-depth tracking
architecture_changes: server.ts: buildLayoutContract() private method — derives structural spec from ch1, ch19, ch21, ch22, ch23 chromosomes. Includes implementation_tier mandate: civilization tier = framework app, ecosystem tier = progressive enhancement.
Response shape: html and html_context fields removed. layout_contract and genome_layer_guide fields added.

pattern-detector.ts: ForbiddenPattern gains optional skipIfInBlock?: RegExp. isInsideBlock() helper walks backwards from match position counting braces, tests 300-char block header. detect() skips matches where skipIfInBlock matches enclosing block header.
no_reduced_motion and infinite_loop_animation patterns gain skipIfInBlock: /prefers-reduced-motion:\s*no-preference/i.
generate_design_brief.ts: ch26_color_system?.secondary and cs.semantic?.success?.hue optional chaining — defensive against partial chromosomes.

pipeline.extraction_layer: LLM analyzes content → extracts StructuralProps (10 behavioral questions) + 8 trait vectors + sector + archetype in a single call
pipeline.sequencing_layer: SHA-256 hash + traits → 32 L1 chromosomes → 11 L2 chromosomes → 10 L3 chromosomes (53 total, three-layer chain)
pipeline.constraint_layer: Epistasis rules enforce cross-chromosome consistency. Forbidden hue ranges enforce sector color psychology. biasedPick prevents archetype collapse.
pipeline.generation_layer: CSS custom properties + layout_contract + WebGL + SVG biomarker + organism specs + civilization architecture

l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch0_sector: Primary/secondary sector classification with brand weight
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch5_color_primary: Primary hue/saturation/lightness + darkModeHex (58–74% lightness for dark surfaces)
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch23_content_depth: Section flags (hasHero, hasFeatures, hasCTA, hasFAQ, hasTestimonials) + estimated section count
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch25_copy_engine: Tone and messaging strategy + LLM-extracted copy (headline, CTA, tagline, social proof strings)
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch26_color_system: Complete color system with harmonies, semantic palette, and contrast ratios
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch27_motion_choreography: Animation timing, stagger patterns, page transitions
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch28_iconography: Icon style, stroke weight, set consistency, CDN
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch30_state_topology: State management pattern — overridden by L3 civ_ch2_governance.model
l1_design_genome_32_chromosomes.ch31_routing_pattern: Routing architecture — overridden by L3 civ_ch7_knowledge.model

l2_ecosystem_genome_11_chromosomes.eco_ch1_biome: Environment type (16 types: temperate/hydrothermal/tundra/etc.) — biased by L1 dark+metallic → hydrothermal
l2_ecosystem_genome_11_chromosomes.eco_ch2_energy: Energy source (photosynthetic/chemosynthetic/predatory/mixotrophic/etc.) — drives archetype gravity in L3
l2_ecosystem_genome_11_chromosomes.eco_ch3_symbiosis: Symbiosis depth (mutualistic/commensal/parasitic/competitive) — drives governance gravity in L3
l2_ecosystem_genome_11_chromosomes.eco_ch4_trophic: Trophic structure — cascade depth drives fauna prey count
l2_ecosystem_genome_11_chromosomes.eco_ch5_succession: Succession stage — organism maturity and complexity profile
l2_ecosystem_genome_11_chromosomes.eco_ch11_mutation: Mutation rate — entropy multiplier for organism variation

l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch1_archetype: Civilization archetype (30 types: tribal→singularity) — biased by L2 energy source
l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch3_economics: Economic model — drives component exchange patterns
l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch4_technology: Technology level — rendering and interaction sophistication
l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch5_culture: Cultural character — visual philosophy of the civilization
l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch6_resilience: Resilience profile — error handling and degradation patterns
l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch8_expansion: Expansion pattern — how the application grows over time
l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch9_age: Civilization age — maturity of patterns and conventions used
l3_civilization_genome_10_chromosomes.civ_ch10_fragility: Fragility score — technical debt tolerance and constraint strictness

epistasis_rules.description: Cross-chromosome constraint enforcement — chromosomes are not independent; they co-evolve
examples: High informationDensity + High temporalUrgency → Low playfulness (serious data work)
Warm color primary → Cool background (contrast balance)
Dashboard archetype → No decorative animations (functional focus)
Inter font + Blue gradient = FORBIDDEN (slop pattern detected)
L2 mutualistic symbiosis → L3 federated governance → ch30_state_topology overridden to 'distributed'
L2 predatory energy → L3 warrior archetype → military design philosophy applied

complexity_tiers.note: 14 tiers across two domains — ecosystem (0.00–0.80) and civilization (0.81–1.00). Tier is computed from StructuralProps, not vocabulary.

ecosystem.abiotic_0.00–0.10: No life — environment only. Layout contract emitted, organism counts are zero.
ecosystem.prokaryotic_0.11–0.22: First cells — minimal structure. Atomic tokens established, few microbial components.
ecosystem.protist_0.23–0.33: Unicellular eukaryotes — basic interaction. Atoms + first interaction states.
ecosystem.bryophyte_0.34–0.44: Non-vascular flora — simple containers. Cards, modals, basic layout components.
ecosystem.vascular_flora_0.45–0.56: Rooted, complex internal structure. Growing components with state, dropdowns, data displays.
ecosystem.invertebrate_fauna_0.57–0.65: First fauna, simple nervous systems. Tables, wizards, paginated lists.
ecosystem.ectotherm_fauna_0.66–0.73: Cold-blooded mobile consumers. Complex multi-step flows, editors, data-dense components.
ecosystem.endotherm_fauna_0.74–0.80: Warm-blooded, all 32 L1 chromosomes active. Full organism ecosystem — all 38 organisms active.

civilization.tribal_0.81–0.86: First civilisation tier — CivilizationGenerator activates. useState, single/multi-page routing, flat/semantic tokens.
civilization.city_state_0.87–0.91: Organised settlement. Shared context state, protected routing, semantic tokens. Command palette unlocked.
civilization.nation_state_0.92–0.94: Unified governance. Zustand store, platform routing (shell + lazy remotes), component-level tokens.
civilization.empire_0.95–0.96: Distributed authority. Zustand + persist middleware, platform/federated routing, governed token system.
civilization.network_0.97–0.98: Post-geographic coordination. Distributed/federated state, Module Federation, cross-system tokens.
civilization.singularity_0.99–1.00: Convergence — all systems unified. Federated state + event bus, full Module Federation, cross-system token graph.

gap_1_ai_slop.solution: Mathematical constraints prevent convergence on mediocrity

gap_2_sector_appropriateness.problem: Healthcare and crypto trading dashboard get the same 'high information density' trait but need completely different heroes, trust signals, colors
gap_2_sector_appropriateness.solution: Sector-aware chromosome generation with industry-specific psychology
gap_2_sector_appropriateness.mechanism: 23 sectors with forbidden hue ranges, hero type weightings, trust signal requirements

gap_3_decoration_vs_function.problem: Tools always generate decorative 3D elements (100% of outputs) when real sites use them <1%
gap_3_decoration_vs_function.solution: Hero type chromosome with 11 functional variants, 3D only when appropriate
gap_3_decoration_vs_function.mechanism: ch19_hero_type selects from: product_ui, stats_counter, trust_authority, configurator_3d, etc.

gap_4_color_psychology.problem: Fixed hue ranges trap every sector into the same narrow palette — all tech products got blue-purple (hue 240–270°) regardless of seed
gap_4_color_psychology.solution: Sector-defined forbiddenRanges replace hueRange — sectors define what's psychologically wrong, hash selects freely from everything else
gap_4_color_psychology.mechanism: Technology has no forbidden ranges (Cloudflare=orange, GitHub=dark, Stripe=purple — all correct). Healthcare forbids blood red + magenta [0–20°, 320–360°]. Food forbids cold corporate blue [220–280°]. Two different tech products with different seeds always get different hues.

gap_5_reproducibility.problem: AI-generated designs cannot be recreated consistently
gap_5_reproducibility.solution: SHA-256 hash of seed = deterministic output
gap_5_reproducibility.mechanism: Same seed → Same DNA forever. Different seed → Different DNA guaranteed.

gap_6_llm_cost.problem: Multiple LLM calls per generation expensive
gap_6_llm_cost.solution: Unified analysis: single call returns structural props + traits + sector + archetype + copy
gap_6_llm_cost.mechanism: Consolidated prompt with structured JSON output, 50% API cost reduction. offline: true = 0 API calls.

gap_7_font_templating.problem: Hardcoded font lists of 6–15 per provider meant every product in the same sector-charge category drew from the same small pool — font slop alongside the color slop the tool was built to prevent
gap_7_font_templating.solution: Live font catalogs fetched from provider APIs at runtime — pool reflects the full catalog, not a curated subset
gap_7_font_templating.mechanism: FontCatalogService with 24h cache. Fontshare (~100 fonts), Google Fonts (1,000+), Bunny (mirrors Google). 'none' provider for self-managed loading. No API key required for any.

gap_8_single_layer_genome.problem: A design genome told you what it should look like (colors, fonts, motion) but not what kind of system it is. Two products with the same complexity score got the same architecture direction regardless of coupling or domain. The genome was a visual brief, not a living system.
gap_8_single_layer_genome.solution: Three-layer SHA-256 genome chain. L1 sequences design character. L2 sequences ecological character (how components relate). L3 sequences civilizational character (how the application is structured). Each layer derived from the previous via sha256(parentHash).
gap_8_single_layer_genome.mechanism: Predecessor chromosome VALUES gravity-bias successor selections. Dark+metallic design → hydrothermal ecosystem. Mutualistic symbiosis → federated governance → ch30_state_topology overridden to 'distributed'. The visual design literally grows into an application architecture.

gap_9_vocabulary_bias.problem: Complexity tier was computed partly from keywords. 'Dashboard', 'platform', 'system' boosted tier. 'Tool', 'app', 'tracker' did not. A complex product described simply got the wrong tier — and the wrong component depth, state management pattern, and routing architecture.
gap_9_vocabulary_bias.solution: StructuralProps: 10 binary/count questions about product behavior. computeComplexityFromStructure() maps answers deterministically. Keyword boost removed entirely.
gap_9_vocabulary_bias.mechanism: LLM answers behavioral questions (realtimeState, sensitiveData, multiRole, financialTransactions, screenCount, etc.) regardless of vocabulary. The mapping to tier is deterministic code — consistent across providers, models, and prompt variations.

gap_10_scaffold_as_template.problem: generate_design_genome returned an HTML scaffold. Agents anchored on it — every site inherited the same nav → hero → features → CTA → footer skeleton. The design tool was producing template lock-in from within its own response. A second gap: three genome layers were returned with no field-to-concern mapping, so agents used L1 for everything and discarded L2 and L3.
gap_10_scaffold_as_template.solution: layout_contract replaces HTML scaffold. genome_layer_guide explicitly maps each layer to its implementation concern. suggested_next specifies which genome field to pass to each tool.
gap_10_scaffold_as_template.mechanism: layout_contract is a JSON structural spec derived from genome chromosomes — not HTML to copy-paste. Agents implement structure from scratch. genome_layer_guide labels L1 (CSS), L2 (component hierarchy), L3 (state/routing). No two agents implementing the same layout_contract produce the same markup.

struggle_1_naive_extraction.example: Looking for 'healthcare' in text and assigning healthcare sector
struggle_1_naive_extraction.failure: Couldn't capture nuance: 'billing portal for high-net-worth patients' needs healthcare+fintech blend
struggle_1_naive_extraction.solution: LLM-based semantic extraction with 8 trait vectors

struggle_2_hardcoded_values.problem: Grid values and colors hardcoded in generators
struggle_2_hardcoded_values.solution: All values now derive from chromosomes (ch9_grid for layout, ch5_color for palette)

struggle_3_3d_everywhere.discovery: Analysis of 111 real sites showed <1% use decorative 3D
struggle_3_3d_everywhere.breakthrough: Hero type chromosome: 3D only for automotive configurators, not Mayo Clinic

struggle_4_llm_fallbacks.problem: System provided 'neutral' fallbacks when LLM failed
struggle_4_llm_fallbacks.issue: Neutral values (0.5 for all traits) produce generic output
struggle_4_llm_fallbacks.decision: HARD ERROR if no API key. HARD ERROR if LLM fails after 3 retries. No fallbacks = forced quality.

struggle_5_separate_calls.breakthrough: Single analyze() call returns {structural, traits, sector, archetype, copy} in one JSON response

struggle_6_url_extraction_stub.problem: extract_genome_from_url was a stub requiring manual CSS paste
struggle_6_url_extraction_stub.failure: Users had to scrape CSS manually — poor UX, not production-ready
struggle_6_url_extraction_stub.breakthrough: Triple backend: Playwright for JS sites, Scrapy for pros, Native fetch fallback

struggle_7_non_deterministic_seeds.problem: Date.now() in seeds meant same input produced different outputs
struggle_7_non_deterministic_seeds.breakthrough: Content-hash based seeds — same input = same output forever

struggle_8_hardcoded_generators.problem: 12 hardcoded component generators (790 lines) for each component type
struggle_8_hardcoded_generators.failure: Adding new component types required new generator functions
struggle_8_hardcoded_generators.breakthrough: Single unified component generator: generate(purpose, elements, layout, complexity)

struggle_9_mutation_gaps.problem: Only 20 of 32 chromosomes had mutation handlers — 12 were static
struggle_9_mutation_gaps.impact: Limited design space exploration, constrained breeding
struggle_9_mutation_gaps.breakthrough: Full coverage: mutation handlers for ch0, ch1, ch2, ch9–ch25

struggle_10_hue_prison.problem: hueRange: [240, 270] for technology meant every tech product got blue-purple — two different products under the same sector looked identical
struggle_10_hue_prison.impact: The exact problem the tool was built to prevent was happening in the color system itself
struggle_10_hue_prison.discovery: Generated 3 tech sites with different seeds, compared screenshots — all had hue ≈ 245°–265°
struggle_10_hue_prison.breakthrough: forbiddenRanges inverts the constraint. Define what's psychologically wrong (not what's right). Hash picks freely from what remains. Technology: no forbidden ranges at all.

struggle_11_dark_mode_invisibility.problem: Primary color generated at lightness 22–35%. On dark backgrounds (5–10% lightness), primary buttons were near-invisible
struggle_11_dark_mode_invisibility.impact: Dark mode sites had invisible CTAs — fundamental usability failure
struggle_11_dark_mode_invisibility.breakthrough: darkModeLightness = clamp(lightness + 0.35, 0.58, 0.74). Every genome now ships a dark-safe interactive variant alongside the base primary.

struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.problem: 14 tools exposed; agents called generate_component and generate_preview to build UIs, treating the tool as a code generator
struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.impact: Tool was generating TSX that contradicted the agent's design intent. generate_component's output competed with the genome's direction.
struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.discovery: The tool's mandate is design direction, not implementation. When a tool generates code, it skips the agent's judgment.
struggle_12_tool_identity_crisis.breakthrough: Removed 6 tools. Added suggested_next to genome response so agents know the workflow. Ecosystem + civilization return specs not code by default.

struggle_13_font_provider_crash.problem: extract_genome_from_url returned headingFont: 'satoshi'. Agent called generate_design_genome with font_provider: 'google' — Satoshi is on Fontshare, not Google Fonts.
struggle_13_font_provider_crash.failure: Tool looked up 'satoshi' from Google's catalog → got undefined → .id on undefined → TypeError crash. The agent had done everything right — the tool failed to handle the mismatch.
struggle_13_font_provider_crash.the_deeper_issue: Even with the crash fixed, the right answer wasn't 'add Satoshi to the Google list.' That's still templating. Static font lists create font slop: every similar product draws from the same small pool.
struggle_13_font_provider_crash.breakthrough: FontCatalogService fetches live catalogs at runtime. Pool went from 6–15 curated fonts to 100–1,000+ live provider fonts.

struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.problem: The original biasedPick used rotation + clamp. For an 8-option array with clamp window=2, gravity values 4, 5, 6, and 7 all produced the same +2 shift — warrior archetype (4), democratic (5), industrial (6), emergent (7) were indistinguishable.
struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.discovery: Six healthcare seeds: mercantile archetype 3 times. Traced: gravity=4 and gravity=6 were producing identical byte distributions.
struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.breakthrough: Replaced rotation+clamp with weighted probability. gravity is a TARGET INDEX. Weight: target=4×, target±1=2×, others=1×. Every distinct target produces a genuinely distinct distribution.
struggle_14_biasedpick_gravity_collapse.impact: Applied to ecosystem-sequencer.ts and civilization-sequencer.ts. Also fixed gravity holes: chemosynthetic→scientific, mixotrophic→maritime in archetypeGravity.

struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.problem: After fixing biasedPick, still saw edge style 'soft' repeating within sectors. Three simultaneous root causes.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.root_cause_1_edge: generateEdge() used playfulness directly: maxRadius × playfulness. At playfulness=0.2 (fintech), max radius=6.4. Organic threshold hardcoded at 16. With max 6, organic was geometrically impossible — every fintech site was soft by construction.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.root_cause_2_motion: generateMotion() selected physics from sector profile then returned. No hash byte influenced selection within the sector. Same sector, 100 different seeds — all got identical physics.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.root_cause_3_biome: Biome byte was still b[0]>>4 (nibble, 0–15). New biasedPick maps rawByte via (rawByte/256)*total. With rawByte max=15 and total≈21, positions 16–20 were unreachable — 5 biomes blocked.
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.fixes: Edge: effectivePlayfulness = Math.max(0.3, playfulness). Motion: b(30)>0.75 deviates to adjacent physics (25% of seeds). Biome: changed b[0]>>4 to b[0].
struggle_15_chromosome_clustering_root_c.verification: 10-seed distribution test across 5 sectors — no single-value lock-in.

struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.problem: The LLM was told to 'BOOST COMPLEXITY when intent includes these keywords: +0.20 dashboard, +0.18 platform...' Same product described differently got different tiers — different component depth, state management, routing architecture.
struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.the_deeper_issue: The 8 trait vectors were doing two jobs: driving visual design character AND computing complexity tier. A product can be architecturally complex but visually minimal — collapsing both into floating-point vectors caused keyword-bias in both.
struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.solution: StructuralProps: 10 binary/count questions about product BEHAVIOR. computeComplexityFromStructure() deterministic. Keyword boost removed entirely from buildPrompt.
struggle_16_vocabulary_complexity_bias.verified: Live test: 'a tool doctors use to check patient vitals' → realtimeState=true → 0.913 city_state. 'Patient portal' → realtimeState=false → 0.763 endotherm_fauna. Plain behavioral description scored HIGHER because describing doctor behavior correctly implies real-time monitoring.

struggle_17_scaffold_convergence.problem: generate_design_genome returned an HTML scaffold. Every agent that received it anchored on the structure — nav at top, hero next, trust grid, features, CTA, footer. Even when the genome's ch19_hero_type specified 'stat_forward' or 'editorial', agents just filled content into the nav→hero→features skeleton. The tool that was supposed to eliminate templates was generating one.
struggle_17_scaffold_convergence.discovery: Agent in Cursor built a site. Different seed, different genome. Same structure. Third build — same skeleton again. Not an agent problem: all three received the same HTML scaffold and all three treated it as a starting point.
struggle_17_scaffold_convergence.the_second_problem: genome_layer_guide was missing. Three genomes returned per call (L1/L2/L3) with no field-to-concern map. Agents used L1 for everything. L2 ecosystemGenome (which controls component hierarchy and organism counts) and L3 civilizationGenome (which controls state topology and routing) were silently discarded on every call.
struggle_17_scaffold_convergence.breakthrough: HTML scaffold → layout_contract (JSON spec derived from genome chromosomes). genome_layer_guide added to response. No markup = no template to anchor on. Two agents given the same layout_contract will produce structurally different implementations. L2 and L3 now have explicit field labels — agents know what each genome controls.

technical_achievements.determinism: Same seed produces identical hash and genome across all three layers — verified across 1,000+ seeds
technical_achievements.uniqueness: ~7.22 × 10^17 perceptibly distinct L1 design genomes. ~6.66 × 10^36 distinct three-layer genomes. forbiddenRanges ensures two seeds in the same sector produce different hues. biasedPick weighted probability ensures distinct gravity values produce distinct distributions.
technical_achievements.design_space: 2^256 ≈ 1.16 × 10^77 possible seeds — greater than atoms in the observable universe (~10^80). 720 quadrillion perceptibly distinct L1 designs from chromosome combinatorics alone. Over 6.6 × 10^36 with all three genome layers.
technical_achievements.three_layer_chain: SHA-256 chain: seed → DesignGenome (L1, 32 chr) → EcosystemGenome (L2, 11 chr) → CivilizationGenome (L3, 10 chr). 53 chromosomes total. Predecessor values gravity-bias successor selections.
technical_achievements.vocabulary_invariant_complexity: StructuralProps: 10 binary/count behavioral questions. computeComplexityFromStructure() deterministic. Same product described differently → same structural answers → same tier.
technical_achievements.no_html_scaffold: layout_contract replaces HTML scaffold. Structural spec derived from genome chromosomes, not fixed markup. genome_layer_guide maps L1/L2/L3 to CSS/hierarchy/architecture respectively. Agents implement without a template to anchor on.
technical_achievements.gravity_biasing: biasedPick weighted probability: target=4×, adjacent=2×, others=1×. Every distinct gravity produces a distinct distribution. Gravity holes filled for chemosynthetic and mixotrophic energy sources.
technical_achievements.edge_diversity: effectivePlayfulness = Math.max(0.3, playfulness). Proportional organicThreshold. All three styles reachable at every playfulness level. Verified across 5 sectors.
technical_achievements.motion_diversity: 25% of seeds deviate to adjacent physics via b(30) > 0.75. Same sector, different seeds, genuinely different motion.
technical_achievements.constraint_enforcement: Epistasis rules prevent 8 known slop patterns. Sector forbidden ranges prevent hue prison. biasedPick prevents archetype collapse.
technical_achievements.sector_coverage: 23 sectors with forbidden hue ranges, hero type weightings, trust signal requirements
technical_achievements.offline_mode: offline: true skips LLM. Falls back to trait-based complexity scoring (structural unavailable). Hash-based trait inference.
technical_achievements.multi_provider: 6 LLM providers: Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, HuggingFace
technical_achievements.test_coverage: 49 production tests: determinism, uniqueness, sector-awareness, epistasis, ecosystem-civilization bridge, pattern detection. All passing.
technical_achievements.url_extraction: Triple backend: Playwright (JS sites), Scrapy (pro crawling), Native fetch (zero deps)
technical_achievements.css_validation: AST-based genome-aware validation with drift scoring 0–100
technical_achievements.dark_mode_safety: Every genome ships darkModeHex (lightness 58–74%). CSS var --color-primary-interactive for buttons on dark backgrounds.
technical_achievements.explainability: Every tool response includes a _report field: genome_report, ecosystem_report, civilization_report
technical_achievements.workflow_guidance: suggested_next with exact genome field per tool + seed convention for hash chain continuity
technical_achievements.tool_surface: 8 tools with STEP-prefixed descriptions. Agents know workflow order without documentation.
technical_achievements.zero_hardcoding: All values derived from SHA-256 hash and LLM extraction — no magic numbers, no placeholder fallbacks
technical_achievements.complete_page_css: CSSGenerator covers every section: navigation, hero variants, trust grid, social proof, features, FAQ, CTA, footer
technical_achievements.api_optimization: Single LLM call returns structural props + traits + sector + copy. offline: true = 0 API calls.
technical_achievements.dynamic_font_catalog: Live font catalogs fetched at runtime — Fontshare (~100), Google Fonts (1,000+), Bunny (mirrors Google). 24h in-memory cache.
technical_achievements.font_provider_breadth: 4 font providers: google, bunny, fontshare, none

real_world_usage.dogfooding: We eat our own cooking — the genome powers its own generation with generated colors, fonts, SVG biomarkers
real_world_usage.api_integration: MCP server integrates with Cursor, Claude Desktop, Windsurf, and any MCP-compatible client
real_world_usage.workflow: Designer describes intent → MCP generates DNA → AI builds UI using constraints
real_world_usage.mcp_installation: genome MCP can be installed globally via `npx @obasoft/genome-mcp` or from local source for development

future_roadmap.v1_1_0_visual_editor: Web UI for breeding genomes: 'warmer colors', 'more density' sliders with live preview
future_roadmap.v1_2_0_design_to_code: Figma/Sketch plugin: extract design → generate DNA → export code
future_roadmap.v1_3_0_a11y_auditor: WCAG 2.2 AA/AAA automated compliance checking with remediation
future_roadmap.v1_4_0_real_time_collaboration: Multiplayer genome editing, versioned design systems
future_roadmap.v1_5_0_perf_budget: Core Web Vitals optimization — automatic performance tuning
future_roadmap.v2_0_0_generative_components: Components generated on-demand from ecosystem organism specs, content, and context

philosophical_position.on_templates: Templates are dead ends. They give you the answer without teaching you the logic.
philosophical_position.on_ai_design: AI shouldn't replace designers — it should give them superpowers. Permutations is the constraint layer that lets AI be creative without being random.
philosophical_position.on_uniqueness: Uniqueness for its own sake is decoration. Uniqueness within constraints is design.
philosophical_position.on_mathematics: Math doesn't constrain creativity — it enables it. The infinite comes from finite rules.
philosophical_position.on_slop: Slop isn't bad because it's common. It's bad because it's thoughtless. Permutations forces thoughtfulness through constraints.
philosophical_position.on_specs_vs_scaffolds: A scaffold tells you what to build and where to put it. A spec tells you what it needs to do and what constraints it must satisfy. Scaffolds produce identical buildings. Specs produce architecture.

cosmic_analogy.title: Permutations as a Universe
cosmic_analogy.subtitle: From infinite possibility to singularity civilization

the_design_universe.description: The SHA-256 address space (2^256 ≈ 10^77 possible seeds) is our universe — vaster than atoms in the observable cosmos (~10^80)
the_design_universe.comparison: Just as the universe's 10^80 atoms arrange into 10^50+ distinct molecular configurations, Permutations' 10^77 seeds arrange into 10^36+ distinct three-layer design systems
the_design_universe.implication: Every design that will ever exist is already mathematically defined, waiting to be discovered via its seed

planetary_bodies.description: Each seed is a planet — a unique point in design space
planetary_bodies.habitable_zone: Sector constraints define which seeds produce viable designs (healthcare planets need trust signals, fintech planets need security)
planetary_bodies.uninhabitable: Some seeds produce slop (Inter + blue gradient) — gas giants: large but lifeless
planetary_bodies.diversity: Like planets orbiting different stars, each seed has unique atmosphere (colors), gravity (rhythm), terrain (topology), geology (texture)

life_exists.description: A living design functions for its purpose

criteria.metabolism: Data flows through components (inputs processed, outputs rendered)
criteria.reproduction: Component patterns replicate with variation (design system propagation)

life_exists.vs_templates: Templates are fossils — preserved but dead. Living designs breathe, respond, evolve.

evolution.mechanism: Mutation of seed → variation → selection by constraints

process.step_2: Chromosomes mutate within sector bounds (hue ±30°, not red→green in healthcare)
process.step_3: Constraint solver selects viable offspring (viability score > 0.7)
process.step_4: User selection drives next generation ('warmer colors', 'more density')

evolution.speciation: Dashboards and portfolios diverged from common ancestor (high information density) but adapted to different environments (scanning vs browsing)
evolution.convergent_evolution: Healthcare and banking both evolved trust signals independently — similar environmental pressure (user vulnerability) produces similar solutions

advancement.thresholds: Complexity isn't linear — it phase-transitions at critical points

great_filters.0.34: From abiotic to life — first bryophyte containers emerge at this threshold
great_filters.0.81: From ecosystem to civilization — tribal tier activates CivilizationGenerator
great_filters.0.97: From empire to network — Module Federation routing and cross-system token graph required

characteristics: Components generate on-demand based on content
Color palette adapts to user mood/time of day
Layout reconfigures for individual cognitive preferences
Motion responds to user's current task context
Accessibility anticipates needs before they're expressed
maximum_complexity.fermi_paradox: If advanced complexity is possible, why haven't we seen it? Because current AI tools default to mediocrity. Permutations is the SETI program for design — listening for signals of intelligent design systems.
maximum_complexity.drake_equation_for_design:
N = R* × fp × ne × fl × fi × fc × L
Where:
R* = rate of design requests
fp = fraction with viable seeds
ne = seeds per sector
fl = fraction that achieve life (viability > 0.7)
fi = fraction that achieve intelligence (complexity > 0.70)
fc = fraction that achieve civilization (complexity > 0.85)
L = lifetime of the design system

closing_thought:
We didn't build another design tool. We built a genetic framework for digital aesthetics. The 53 chromosomes across three layers are the letters of an alphabet. The SHA-256 hash is the seed. The constraints are the grammar. And every website generated is a unique poem — functional, appropriate, and unmistakably itself.

The numbers: 2^256 possible seeds. 720 quadrillion perceptibly distinct L1 designs. 6.6 × 10^36 distinct three-layer genomes when L2 (ecosystem) and L3 (civilization) are included. The observable universe has ~10^80 atoms. We have ~10^77 seeds. Every designer using Permutations is an astronomer, discovering new planets in a design space that dwarfs the cosmos.

No templates. No slop. Only math.
